---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_macro_panel.csv")
  
  # Variables
  entity_var: "district"
  time_var: "year"
  value_var: "literacy_rate"
  
  # Policy Narrative Focus
  highlight_entity: "Lahore"
  metric_name: "Literacy Rank"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "League Table: Bump Chart"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
if (!require("ggrepel")) install.packages("ggrepel")
library(ggrepel)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for the specific variables required
  drop_na(!!sym(params$time_var), !!sym(params$entity_var), !!sym(params$value_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub and wrap Entity names, and force Time/Value to numeric types
  mutate(
    Entity_Clean = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$entity_var)))),
    Entity = str_wrap(Entity_Clean, width = 15),
    Time = as.numeric(!!sym(params$time_var)),
    Value = as.numeric(!!sym(params$value_var))
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Group by year to establish who is in 1st, 2nd, 3rd place, etc.
  group_by(Time) %>%
  mutate(
    # Strict tie-breaker for vertical spacing on the chart
    Rank = row_number(desc(Value)),
    # Mathematical detection: Is this exact score duplicated in this year?
    is_tied = duplicated(Value) | duplicated(Value, fromLast = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 4. Create safe styling rules and dynamic text labels
  mutate(
    target_clean = str_to_title(str_trim(params$highlight_entity)),
    is_highlight = ifelse(Entity_Clean == target_clean, TRUE, FALSE),
    # If tied, append an asterisk to the name
    Display_Name = ifelse(is_tied, paste0(Entity, "*"), Entity)
  )

# 5. Isolate the starting and ending points to draw direct text labels
start_labels <- plot_data %>% filter(Time == min(Time))
end_labels <- plot_data %>% filter(Time == max(Time))

# 6. Dynamically generate the caption only if a tie actually exists in the dataset
caption_text <- ifelse(
  any(plot_data$is_tied), 
  "Generated via CERP Analytics Template | * Indicates a mathematical tie in score, rank forced for visibility.", 
  "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
)
```


```{r plot-bump}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Time, y = Rank, group = Entity)) +
  
  # Draw the trajectory lines
  geom_line(aes(
    color = is_highlight, 
    linewidth = is_highlight, 
    alpha = is_highlight
  )) +
  
  # Draw the specific rank points for each year
  geom_point(aes(
    color = is_highlight, 
    size = is_highlight
  )) +
  
  # Left-side labels (Start) — repelled vertically so tied ranks never overprint
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = start_labels, aes(label = Display_Name, color = is_highlight),
    direction = "y", hjust = 1, nudge_x = -0.15,
    fontface = "bold", size = 4, family = cerp_font_caption,
    box.padding = 0.12, segment.color = "#b7c9d3", segment.size = 0.3, seed = 26
  ) +

  # Right-side labels (End) — same protection
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = end_labels, aes(label = Display_Name, color = is_highlight),
    direction = "y", hjust = 0, nudge_x = 0.15,
    fontface = "bold", size = 4, family = cerp_font_caption,
    box.padding = 0.12, segment.color = "#b7c9d3", segment.size = 0.3, seed = 26
  ) +
  
  # Apply conditional styling
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]), "FALSE" = "#B0B0B0")) +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 2.5, "FALSE" = 1)) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 1, "FALSE" = 0.5)) +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 5, "FALSE" = 3)) +
  
  # Reverse the Y-axis so Rank #1 is at the top of the chart
  scale_y_reverse(breaks = 1:max(plot_data$Rank)) +
  
  # FIX: Expanded the X-axis margins (40% left, 20% right) to prevent long names from clipping
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(plot_data$Time), 
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.4, 0.2))) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip everything unnecessary from the plot
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", color = cerp_cols[["text"]]),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste(params$metric_name, "Trajectory")),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else (paste("Tracking regional shifts over time. Highlighting the performance of", params$highlight_entity, ".")),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else (NULL),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else ("Rank"),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
